name: Chicken Breasts with Green Coriander (Cilantro)
recipeInstructions:
1. Skin the chicken breasts and dust them with a little salt and pepper.
2. Lay each chicken breast on a square of baking parchment, flatten lightly with the palm of your hand, and place a sprig of coriander on top.
3. Fold the sides of the parchment over the meat, excluding as much air as possible, and roll up to make a neat flat parcel.
4. Carefully wrap the parcel in foil to create a waterproof package.
5. Trim and scrub the carrots. Measure the olive oil into a small saucepan and add a seasoning of salt and pepper. Coarsely chop about 1 tablespoon of coriander leaves (a little less for beginners, a little more for addicts) and reserve separately.
6. About 20 minutes before serving, start cooking. Steam the baby carrots until just tender.
7. Drop the chicken parcels, seam side up, into a large pan of boiling water and cook for 13 to 18 minutes depending on the size of the breasts. Ensure the liquid is barely bubbling while the chicken cooks.
8. Transfer the parcels, still wrapped, to a low oven for 5 minutes to let the meat set.
9. Blot the carrots dry and arrange them on a warmed serving dish.
10. Warm the olive oil in a pan. Add just 1 to 2 tablespoons of juices from the cooking water to the warm olive oil.
11. Quickly carve the chicken breasts into thin slices and arrange the meat beside the carrots.
12. Add the lemon juice to the hot olive oil and immediately pour over the chicken and carrots.
13. Scatter with chopped coriander and serve straight away with hot crusty bread to mop up the juices.
